channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Young professionals working in heritage conservation, historic gardens, architecture, landscape design, and cultural preservation now have an exciting global opportunity to showcase their work through the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme in Suzhou, China. Organized by the UNESCO Regional Office for East Asia and the World Heritage Institute of Training and Research […]

The post UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young professionals working in heritage conservation, historic gardens, architecture, landscape design, and cultural preservation now have an exciting global opportunity to showcase their work through the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme in Suzhou, China.

Organized by the UNESCO Regional Office for East Asia and the World Heritage Institute of Training and Research for the Asia-Pacific Region (WHITR-AP Suzhou), this prestigious programme is calling for innovative, youth-led projects focused on historic gardens, living heritage conservation, sustainability, and public engagement.

The 2026 edition is themed:

“Historic Gardens: Living Heritage Conservation and Public Engagement.”

Selected participants will participate in an exclusive regional workshop in Suzhou, China, where they will engage in interdisciplinary dialogue, present their work, exchange knowledge with experts, and contribute to regional discussions on heritage conservation and sustainable cultural development.

This fully funded opportunity is designed to empower young professionals and practitioners who are actively preserving cultural landscapes while integrating sustainability, biodiversity conservation, traditional craftsmanship, and community engagement into their projects.

Applications are open until June 30, 2026.

About the UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation Programme

The Young Masters for Heritage Conservation initiative recognizes that youth are critical custodians of cultural and natural heritage. Historic gardens, as living heritage systems, represent a unique intersection of architecture, nature, artistry, culture, and history.

The programme aims to:

  1. Recognize outstanding youth-led contributions to heritage conservation
  2. Promote innovative approaches to preserving historic gardens
  3. Encourage sustainable heritage management practices
  4. Foster regional collaboration and knowledge exchange
  5. Strengthen public engagement with cultural landscapes
  6. Connect heritage conservation with biodiversity and climate resilience
  7. Build a network of emerging heritage professionals across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond

The initiative particularly values projects that combine traditional conservation approaches with contemporary innovation and sustainability.

Why Historic Gardens Matter in Heritage Conservation

Historic gardens are more than beautiful landscapes. They are living expressions of culture, identity, craftsmanship, and ecological wisdom.

These heritage spaces:

  • Reflect the relationship between humans and nature
  • Preserve traditional architectural and horticultural knowledge
  • Support biodiversity and environmental sustainability
  • Serve as cultural gathering spaces
  • Promote tourism, education, and community engagement
  • Contribute to climate resilience and urban sustainability

UNESCO’s programme seeks to preserve these important cultural landscapes while making them relevant and accessible to modern society.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected participants will receive several professional and academic benefits through the programme.

1. Fully Funded Participation in the Suzhou Workshop

Participants selected for the workshop will have major costs covered, including:

  • International travel expenses
  • Accommodation in Suzhou
  • Meals during the programme
  • Local transportation
  • Insurance coverage

This makes the programme highly accessible for young professionals worldwide.

2. International Professional Exposure

Participants will gain visibility within UNESCO’s international heritage network and connect with:

  • Heritage conservation experts
  • Architects and landscape designers
  • Cultural practitioners
  • Policy experts
  • Conservation managers
  • Researchers and academics

3. Knowledge Exchange and Peer Learning

The workshop will provide opportunities for:

  • Collaborative learning
  • Interdisciplinary discussions
  • Sharing innovative conservation models
  • Exploring regional and global heritage challenges
  • Learning sustainable conservation strategies

4. Certificate of Recognition

Participants who complete the programme will receive a:

“Young Masters for Heritage Conservation” Certificate

This certificate can significantly strengthen professional profiles in architecture, heritage conservation, cultural management, and urban sustainability.

5. Exhibition and Project Showcase

Participants’ work may be featured in curated exhibitions showcasing innovative heritage conservation practices.

6. Global Networking Opportunities

The programme creates opportunities to build long-term collaborations with professionals and institutions working in:

  • Heritage conservation
  • Landscape architecture
  • Cultural sustainability
  • Creative industries
  • Urban development
  • Environmental conservation

Workshop Focus Areas

The workshop will explore several important themes related to historic garden conservation.

Key Themes Include:

1. Conservation and Integrated Management

Participants will discuss methods for conserving both:

  • Architectural elements
  • Landscape environments

within historic gardens.

2. Traditional Knowledge and Craftsmanship

The programme emphasizes preserving and revitalizing:

  • Traditional building methods
  • Indigenous horticultural practices
  • Cultural craftsmanship
  • Heritage skills transmission

3. Sustainability and Climate Resilience

Projects focused on:

  • Climate adaptation
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Sustainable resource management
  • Ecological restoration

are strongly encouraged.

4. Public Engagement and Creative Activation

The programme supports innovative strategies that make historic gardens more accessible and engaging for modern audiences.

Examples include:

  • Cultural programming
  • Art installations
  • Educational activities
  • Community engagement initiatives
  • Digital storytelling
  • Interactive exhibitions

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from young professionals and practitioners working in heritage conservation and related creative fields.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Conservation architects
  • Landscape architects
  • Garden designers
  • Traditional building engineers
  • Heritage site managers
  • Cultural landscape practitioners
  • Creative industry professionals
  • Researchers and academics
  • Cultural programmers
  • Designers working on heritage projects

Eligible Projects

Applicants may submit projects related to historic gardens and cultural landscapes.

Examples include:

1. Conservation and Restoration Projects

Projects involving:

  • Garden restoration
  • Preservation of historic landscapes
  • Architectural conservation
  • Cultural landscape management

2. Traditional Knowledge Preservation

Projects integrating:

  • Traditional craftsmanship
  • Indigenous horticultural techniques
  • Sustainable garden practices
  • Traditional ecological knowledge

3. Climate and Biodiversity Projects

Projects promoting:

  • Climate resilience
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Ecological regeneration

4. Community Engagement Projects

Initiatives focused on:

  • Public participation
  • Educational outreach
  • Heritage awareness
  • Community stewardship

5. Creative and Artistic Projects

Projects linking historic gardens with:

  • Art
  • Design
  • Digital storytelling
  • Cultural events
  • Creative interpretation

Important Eligibility Conditions

Projects must:

  • Be completed within the last 10 years
  • Demonstrate measurable impact
  • Show innovation and sustainability
  • Reflect cultural relevance
  • Promote public engagement

Projects do not need to be located within UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on both applicant qualifications and project impact.

Candidate Evaluation Criteria

Applicants should demonstrate:

  1. Commitment to cultural dialogue and international cooperation
  2. Leadership in heritage conservation
  3. Innovation and sustainability
  4. Willingness to contribute to peer learning and collaboration

Project Evaluation Criteria

Projects will be assessed based on:

1. Integration of Culture and Nature

Projects should align with sustainable development and environmental goals.

2. Conservation of Traditional Knowledge

Projects should preserve and transmit cultural practices and heritage skills.

3. Innovation and Creativity

Applications should demonstrate creative conservation approaches and modern solutions.

4. Social Impact

Projects should encourage community participation and public engagement.

5. Replicability

Projects should have potential to inspire similar initiatives elsewhere.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Completed Young Masters Application Form
  2. Project description with images (PDF format)
  3. Recommendation letter (optional but encouraged)
  4. Supporting materials such as:
    • Drawings
    • Photos
    • Research papers
    • Reports
    • Videos
  5. Relevant certifications or awards (if available)

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Application Timeline

Key Dates

  • Applications Open: May 1, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 30, 2026
  • Evaluation Period: July 1–15, 2026
  • Results Announcement: July 16–22, 2026
  • Workshop in Suzhou, China: Late August/Early September 2026

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email to:

jgzx_gjjl@hotmail.com

Applicants are advised to carefully prepare their project materials and ensure all supporting documentation is included before submission.

Why You Should Apply

The UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation programme is a rare opportunity for emerging heritage leaders to gain international recognition while contributing to the future of sustainable cultural preservation.

This programme is ideal for young professionals seeking to:

  • Build global networks
  • Showcase impactful conservation work
  • Gain UNESCO recognition
  • Learn from experts
  • Promote sustainable heritage practices
  • Strengthen professional visibility
  • Contribute to global cultural dialogue

As climate change, urbanization, and modernization continue to threaten cultural landscapes, innovative youth-led conservation initiatives are becoming increasingly important worldwide.

Pro Tip for Applicants

Focus your application on measurable impact and storytelling.

UNESCO is looking for projects that not only preserve heritage but also:

  • Engage communities
  • Address sustainability challenges
  • Demonstrate innovation
  • Bridge tradition with modern solutions

Use strong visuals, clear outcomes, and concise narratives to showcase the uniqueness of your work.

Click HERE to learn more about UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China.

Final Thoughts

The UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 programme offers more than a workshop—it is a global platform for emerging leaders shaping the future of cultural and environmental sustainability.

If you are passionate about historic gardens, cultural landscapes, architecture, sustainability, and public engagement, this is an opportunity to showcase your work on an international stage while connecting with some of the world’s leading heritage professionals.

For young architects, designers, conservation practitioners, and cultural innovators, this programme can become a transformative milestone in your professional journey.

Discover more global Fellowship and Climate Action opportunities on OFY.

The post UNESCO Young Masters for Heritage Conservation 2026 in China: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Architects, Heritage Professionals, and Creative Practitioners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) has officially opened applications for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium. This prestigious international job opportunity offers qualified professionals the chance to work at the center of global security, defence innovation, and multinational cooperation. The full-time role is housed within the […]

The post The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) has officially opened applications for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium. This prestigious international job opportunity offers qualified professionals the chance to work at the center of global security, defence innovation, and multinational cooperation.

The full-time role is housed within the NATO International Staff (NATO IS) under the NATO Office of Resources (NOR), specifically in the Air & Space Capabilities (ASC) Unit. Successful candidates will contribute directly to NATO’s defence and missile protection strategies while collaborating with experts from member nations and international agencies.

With a highly competitive monthly salary of €8,273.12 and strong international career growth opportunities, this NATO job opening is one of the top defence and security sector opportunities available in 2026.

Overview of the NATO BMD Capabilities Officer Position

Position Details

  • Position Title: Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities
  • Job ID: 260804
  • Organization: NATO International Staff (NATO IS)
  • Location: Brussels, Belgium
  • Employment Type: Full-time
  • Salary: €8,273.12 per month
  • Grade Level: NATO Grade G17-G20
  • Application Deadline: 15 June 2026
  • Contract Duration: Initial 3-year contract with possible renewal up to 6 years

This role is particularly important within NATO’s strategic defence framework, as ballistic missile threats continue to evolve globally. The selected officer will support the development, implementation, evaluation, and lifecycle management of NATO-funded missile defence capabilities.

About NATO’s Office of Resources (NOR)

The NATO Office of Resources serves as a key advisory body within NATO. It provides:

  • Technical advice
  • Financial analysis
  • Economic assessments
  • Policy guidance
  • Resource allocation expertise

The Office supports NATO leadership, Allies, military authorities, and resource committees in managing military common-funded capabilities effectively.

Within this structure, the Officer for BMD Capabilities plays a critical role in evaluating military requirements, investment planning, operational costs, and workforce implications associated with missile defence systems.

Key Responsibilities of the Officer, BMD Capabilities

Successful applicants will be responsible for a broad range of strategic and technical duties.

1. Technical and Strategic Expertise

The officer will:

  • Evaluate NATO military requirements related to missile defence
  • Assess technical solutions for defence projects
  • Analyze project schedules, performance, costs, and risks
  • Review operation and maintenance budgets
  • Monitor NATO-funded capabilities throughout their lifecycle

2. Policy Development

The role includes contributing to NATO policy frameworks by:

  • Supporting policy development for military common-funded resources
  • Applying NATO implementation and operational policies
  • Advising on resource utilization and programme compliance

3. Stakeholder Coordination

The selected candidate will coordinate with:

  • NATO agencies
  • National defence authorities
  • NATO military authorities
  • International Staff members
  • Resource Committees

The position requires strong diplomatic and organizational skills due to the multinational nature of NATO operations.

4. Representation and Leadership

The officer will:

  • Present technical reports to NATO committees
  • Chair technical meetings
  • Lead project discussions involving multiple stakeholders
  • Represent NATO’s Office of Resources in strategic defence meetings

Salary and Benefits

The NATO Officer, BMD Capabilities position offers an attractive compensation package and international work environment.

Financial Benefits

  • Monthly salary of €8,273.12
  • NATO salary scale benefits and allowances
  • International employment benefits package

Professional Benefits

  • Opportunity to work within a globally respected international organization
  • Exposure to high-level defence and security projects
  • Multinational networking opportunities
  • Participation in strategic defence policy development
  • Career advancement within NATO structures

Work-Life Benefits

NATO also offers:

  • Teleworking arrangements
  • Flexible work schedules (Flexitime)
  • Inclusive and diverse work environment
  • Professional development opportunities

Required Qualifications and Experience

To be eligible for this NATO job opportunity, candidates must meet several educational and professional requirements.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent qualification in:
    • Computer Science
    • Electronic Engineering
    • Closely related science or engineering disciplines

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • At least 5 years of professional experience in:
    • Development of complex systems
    • Procurement programmes
    • Maintenance of defence or technical systems
    • Related technical programme management fields

Technical Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Knowledge of project and programme management methodologies
  • Ability to communicate technical and financial information effectively
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

Language Requirements

Candidates must possess NATO language proficiency levels:

  • Advanced level in English or French
  • Elementary level in the second official NATO language

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, NATO considers the following an advantage:

  • Experience with Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD)
  • Knowledge of Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) systems
  • Understanding of NATO common-funding procedures
  • Familiarity with:
    • Space defence operations
    • Air Surveillance systems
    • Counter-Unmanned Air Systems (C-UAS)

Competencies NATO Is Looking For

NATO seeks professionals who can demonstrate:

  • Analytical thinking
  • Leadership and initiative
  • Team collaboration
  • Organizational awareness
  • Strategic influence
  • High performance standards

These competencies are critical for navigating NATO’s complex multinational working environment.

Contract Details and Career Progression

The successful applicant will initially receive:

  • A 3-year definite duration contract
  • Possibility of renewal for another 3 years

During the renewal period, candidates may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract under NATO policies.

Applicants already seconded from national administrations may also be considered under NATO secondment rules.

Diversity and Inclusion at NATO

NATO strongly promotes workplace diversity and equal opportunity employment.

The organization encourages applications from:

  • Women professionals
  • Candidates from all NATO member nations
  • Individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds
  • Applicants living with disabilities

NATO also provides reasonable accommodation during recruitment and employment processes.

Important Application Information

Applicants must submit their applications through NATO’s official recruitment system.

Before Applying

Candidates should ensure:

  • Their CV is updated
  • Supporting documents are accurate
  • Their Taleo candidate profile is complete

Security and Verification

Successful applicants will undergo:

  • Security clearance checks
  • Medical evaluation
  • Qualification verification
  • Accreditation procedures

Important NATO Recruitment Notice

NATO strictly prohibits the use of plagiarism or generative AI tools in preparing applications.

Applications generated wholly or partially using AI systems, translation tools, or copied materials without proper citation may be rejected automatically.

Applicants are advised to prepare authentic and original applications.

Why This NATO Job Opportunity Matters

This position provides an exceptional opportunity for professionals interested in:

  • International defence cooperation
  • Missile defence systems
  • Security policy
  • Strategic military capabilities
  • Aerospace and defence engineering

Working with NATO places professionals at the forefront of international peace, defence innovation, and multinational collaboration.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the NATO Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities position is:

15 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all eligibility and documentation requirements before submission.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via NATO’s official recruitment platform.

Apply Here

Applicants should carefully follow all instructions provided on the NATO Talent Acquisition Platform.

 

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

 

The post The North Atlantic Treaty Organization Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Officer, Ballistic Missile Defence (BMD) Capabilities Position in Brussels, Belgium appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The environmental sector continues to face significant challenges around racial representation and equitable leadership pathways. Despite increasing awareness about diversity and inclusion, leadership roles within environmental organisations still do not reflect the diversity of wider society. To help address this systemic challenge, Common Purpose and Makani Cambridge have launched Natural Catalysts, a transformative 12-month leadership […]

The post Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The environmental sector continues to face significant challenges around racial representation and equitable leadership pathways. Despite increasing awareness about diversity and inclusion, leadership roles within environmental organisations still do not reflect the diversity of wider society.

To help address this systemic challenge, Common Purpose and Makani Cambridge have launched Natural Catalysts, a transformative 12-month leadership programme designed to reshape how leadership is understood, accessed, practised, and sustained across the environmental sector.

Applications are now open for the Natural Catalysts Programme 2026, an initiative specifically created to support Global Majority Leaders and Senior Leaders working together to create lasting systemic change.

The programme aims to build more equitable, inclusive, and sustainable leadership pathways across environmental organisations in the UK.

What Is the Natural Catalysts Programme?

Natural Catalysts is a leadership development initiative focused on:

  • Racial equity
  • Leadership transformation
  • Organisational change
  • Systems thinking
  • Inclusive environmental leadership

The programme is delivered through a partnership between:

  • Common Purpose
  • Makani Cambridge

It is supported by:

  • Esmée Fairbairn Foundation

Unlike many leadership initiatives that focus solely on individual career progression, Natural Catalysts adopts an ecosystem approach.

This means the programme seeks to influence:

  • Individuals
  • Organisations
  • Leadership structures
  • Sector-wide systems
  • Organisational culture
  • Access to power and decision-making

The programme was developed following a year-long research and co-design process involving:

  • Global Majority Leaders
  • Senior Leaders
  • Environmental organisations
  • Equity practitioners
  • Community stakeholders

Why the Natural Catalysts Programme Matters

The environmental sector has historically struggled with racial representation in leadership positions.

According to the programme organisers, the issue is not simply about talent shortages or recruitment pipelines. Instead, it is deeply connected to:

  • How leadership is defined
  • How opportunities are distributed
  • Organisational power structures
  • Informal access to networks
  • Visibility and sponsorship
  • Systemic inequities

Natural Catalysts seeks to challenge and transform these structures.

The programme recognises that meaningful and lasting change requires:

  • Organisational commitment
  • Shared accountability
  • Cross-level collaboration
  • Honest conversations about race and power
  • Structural change beyond symbolic representation

Programme Vision and Goals

Natural Catalysts is designed to:

  • Increase equitable leadership opportunities
  • Support Global Majority Leaders
  • Encourage institutional accountability
  • Shift leadership culture in the environmental sector
  • Build long-term systemic change

The programme aims to move organisations:

  • From representation to access and influence
  • From mentoring to sponsorship and visibility
  • From individual progression to collective transformation

Who Can Apply?

Natural Catalysts is open to organisations operating within the environmental sector.

Organisations are invited to nominate a “duo” of participants who will complete the programme together.

Required Participant Structure

1. Global Majority Leader

The nominated Global Majority Leader must:

  • Be in a paid role
  • Have lived experience of racialisation in the UK
  • Identify within communities racialised as Black, Asian, Brown, or Arab in the UK context
  • Be operating at mid-management level or above
  • Be navigating leadership pathways within the environmental sector

2. Senior Leader

The Senior Leader must:

  • Be in a paid senior role
  • Hold significant decision-making power
  • Influence organisational direction
  • Be part of senior management or executive leadership

Examples may include:

  • CEOs
  • Directors
  • Senior Management Team members
  • Executive leaders

The organisers emphasise that leadership titles may vary across organisations. What matters most is:

  • Influence
  • Strategic authority
  • Organisational decision-making power

Important Participation Rule

A Global Majority Leader cannot participate without senior leadership involvement.

However:

  • A Senior Leader may participate independently if their organisation does not currently employ a Global Majority Leader.

This structure is intentional because the programme seeks to create organisational change rather than focusing only on individual leadership development.

Additional Participation Opportunity for HR and DEI Professionals

Organisations may also nominate professionals from:

  • Human Resources (HR)
  • Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI)
  • Learning and Development (L&D)

These participants will join a dedicated:

  • Community of Practice

This additional track helps organisations embed learning into policies, systems, and workplace culture.

What Participants Will Learn

The programme explores critical themes around:

  • Leadership
  • Race
  • Power
  • Equity
  • Organisational systems
  • Community-centred change

Participants will:

  • Deepen understanding of power structures
  • Explore leadership beyond hierarchy
  • Learn across lived experiences and roles
  • Build supportive professional communities
  • Strengthen organisational influence
  • Reflect on accountability and responsibility
  • Develop sustainable leadership practices

Programme Structure

Natural Catalysts is a 12-month programme involving:

  • 40 participants total
  • 20 Global Majority Leaders
  • 20 Senior Leaders

The programme combines:

  • In-person residential learning
  • Online sessions
  • Peer communities
  • Collaborative reflection
  • Leadership development
  • Systems-change approaches

Programme Components

1. Online Onboarding Session

The programme begins with a:

  • Two-hour online onboarding session

Participants will:

  • Build initial connections
  • Understand programme expectations
  • Explore themes of leadership and systemic change

2. Global Majority Leaders Track

This specialised leadership track includes:

In-Person Residential

  • Three-day residential gathering
  • Community-building activities
  • Leadership reflection
  • Collective learning experiences

Community of Practice

  • Ten one-hour sessions
  • Peer support and collaborative learning
  • Shared reflection and sense-making

3. Senior Leaders Track

Senior leaders will engage in:

  • Organisational leadership reflection
  • Power and accountability discussions
  • Influence and systems-change learning
  • Cross-sector collaboration

4. Joint Learning Track

Participants from different leadership levels will learn together through:

  • Shared discussions
  • Collaborative exercises
  • Organisational change exploration
  • Leadership practice reflection

5. HR, DEI, and Leadership Professionals Track

This specialised track supports organisational professionals responsible for:

  • Workplace culture
  • Equity systems
  • Inclusion practices
  • Leadership development frameworks

6. Final In-Person Gathering

The programme concludes with a:

  • 24-hour residential gathering

Participants will:

  • Reflect on learning outcomes
  • Share organisational insights
  • Discuss change implementation
  • Celebrate programme completion

Time Commitment

The programme requires meaningful participation and organisational engagement.

Global Majority Leaders

  • 4 in-person days
  • 18 hours online

Senior Leaders

  • 20 hours online
  • 1 in-person day
  • Shadowing and visibility commitments

HR/DEI Participants

  • 4 hours online

The organisers emphasise that this programme is intended to influence participants’ work directly rather than exist alongside it as a separate activity.

Benefits of Participating

Natural Catalysts offers extensive personal, professional, and organisational benefits.

Leadership Development

Participants strengthen:

  • Strategic leadership skills
  • Systems thinking
  • Cross-boundary leadership
  • Organisational influence

Networking and Community

Participants gain:

  • Access to supportive peer networks
  • Cross-sector relationships
  • Collaborative learning communities

Organisational Impact

Organisations benefit from:

  • More inclusive leadership practices
  • Stronger equity frameworks
  • Improved representation pathways
  • Enhanced organisational reflection

Exposure and Visibility

Participants gain:

  • Increased visibility
  • Sponsorship opportunities
  • Access to influential spaces and networks

Sector-Wide Transformation

The programme contributes to:

  • More equitable environmental leadership
  • Sustainable systems change
  • Greater racial representation in leadership roles

Cost and Financial Support

Programme Fee

  • £500 for organisations able to pay

Bursaries

Bursaries are available.

The organisers state clearly that:

  • Financial limitations should not prevent organisations from applying.

Information Webinar

An information webinar will be held for interested applicants.

Webinar Details

  • Date: 23 June 2026
  • Time: 12:00–13:00 BST
  • Platform: Zoom

The webinar will provide:

  • Programme insights
  • Application guidance
  • Opportunity for questions and discussion

About the Organising Partners

Common Purpose

Common Purpose is an international leadership development organisation founded in 1989.

The organisation:

  • Operates in over 100 cities worldwide
  • Has over 130,000 alumni globally
  • Focuses on cross-boundary leadership development
  • Supports collaborative problem-solving across sectors

Makani Cambridge

Makani Cambridge CIC is a community-rooted organisation focused on:

  • Racial equity
  • Leadership
  • Collective care
  • Community-led systems change

Since 2024, Makani has:

  • Delivered more than 70 programmes and events
  • Reached over 1,000 participants across the UK

Their work centres:

  • Lived experience
  • Community-rooted learning
  • Equity-focused systems change

Deadline for Applications

Midnight UK Time, Sunday 12 July 2026

How to Apply

Interested organisations can learn more and apply through the official Common Purpose platform.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The Natural Catalysts Programme represents a significant effort to address long-standing inequities in environmental leadership by moving beyond surface-level diversity initiatives toward meaningful structural change.

Through collaboration between Global Majority Leaders, Senior Leaders, and organisational professionals, the programme seeks to transform how leadership is practised and sustained across the environmental sector.

For organisations committed to racial equity, inclusive leadership, and systems transformation, Natural Catalysts offers a rare opportunity to engage deeply with the realities of power, representation, and organisational change while building lasting networks and practical leadership capacity.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Natural Catalysts 2026: Leadership Programme Advancing Racial Equity in the Environmental Sector Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations WIPO Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland: Paid International Internship Opportunities for Students and Graduates
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland. This prestigious international internship programme offers students and recent graduates from around the world the opportunity to gain professional experience within one of the leading United Nations specialized agencies focused on intellectual property, innovation, creativity, and global development. The […]

The post United Nations WIPO Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland: Paid International Internship Opportunities for Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland. This prestigious international internship programme offers students and recent graduates from around the world the opportunity to gain professional experience within one of the leading United Nations specialized agencies focused on intellectual property, innovation, creativity, and global development.

The WIPO Internship Program 2026 is designed to help young professionals strengthen their academic and professional skills while working in a multicultural international environment at WIPO Headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland, and other WIPO external offices worldwide.

The application deadline for the 2026 WIPO Internship Roster is July 3, 2026.

Selected interns will receive monthly stipends of up to 2,170 Swiss Francs, professional exposure, international networking opportunities, and hands-on work experience across multiple fields including law, economics, technology, communications, translation, project management, administration, intellectual property, and more.

About the WIPO Internship Program

The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to promoting innovation and creativity for the economic, social, and cultural development of all countries through a balanced and effective international intellectual property system.

The WIPO Internship Program supports WIPO’s mission by providing students and graduates with opportunities to:

  • Gain practical international work experience
  • Develop professional competencies
  • Learn about global intellectual property systems
  • Work in multicultural environments
  • Contribute to international development initiatives
  • Engage with global innovation and policy issues

According to WIPO, the programme also benefits the organization by bringing in young talents with fresh perspectives, current academic knowledge, and innovative ideas.

Internship Location

Most internship opportunities are based at:

  • WIPO Headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland

However, internships may also be available at:

  • WIPO external offices outside Geneva

Please note that WIPO internships cannot be conducted remotely. Interns are expected to work full-time onsite.

Internship Categories

The WIPO Internship Program is divided into two major categories depending on the educational level of applicants.

Category I Internship

Eligible Applicants

This category is open to:

  1. Students pursuing a first university degree who are in their third or fourth year of study
  2. Students required to complete internships as part of their academic programme
  3. Recent graduates who completed their first-level university degree within the past two years
  4. Students pursuing advanced degrees who have not completed coursework or thesis

Duration

  • Minimum: 2 months
  • Maximum: 6 months

Stipend

Selected Category I interns receive:

  • 1,645 Swiss Francs per month
  • Local transportation allowance included

This applies to interns assigned to WIPO Headquarters in Geneva.

Category II Internship

Eligible Applicants

This category is open to:

  1. Students pursuing advanced university degrees who have completed coursework but not final thesis
  2. Graduates who obtained advanced degrees within the last two years
  3. Individuals with advanced degrees currently enrolled in further postgraduate studies

Duration

  • Minimum: 2 months
  • Maximum: 12 months

Stipend

Selected Category II interns receive:

  • 2,170 Swiss Francs per month
  • Local transportation allowance included

This applies to interns assigned to WIPO Headquarters in Geneva.

Internship Fields and Areas

WIPO offers internships across a broad range of disciplines and professional sectors.

Internship Areas Include:

  1. Law and Intellectual Property Law
  2. Economics and Statistics
  3. Information Technology
  4. Technical Cooperation Services
  5. Project Administration
  6. Finance
  7. Human Resources
  8. Procurement
  9. Program Planning
  10. Patent and Trademark Examination
  11. Copyright
  12. Translation
  13. Communications
  14. Conference Services
  15. Security
  16. Other administrative and technical areas

This wide variety of internship sectors allows students and graduates from multiple academic backgrounds to apply.

Main Responsibilities of Interns

Intern responsibilities vary depending on the department and assignment.

Generally, interns may:

  • Support ongoing projects and research
  • Assist with policy analysis
  • Contribute to intellectual property initiatives
  • Draft reports and communications
  • Participate in administrative tasks
  • Conduct data analysis
  • Support event coordination
  • Assist in legal or technical research
  • Collaborate with international teams

Specific Terms of Reference are provided by each hiring office.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following conditions:

Educational Requirements

Candidates must:

  • Be currently pursuing a university degree, or
  • Have completed a university degree within the last two years

For undergraduate applicants:

  • Must be in third or fourth year of study, or
  • Be in a programme requiring internship completion

For graduate applicants:

  • Must meet Category II educational requirements

Required Skills

Applicants should possess:

  • Good analytical skills
  • Strong communication abilities
  • Interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments
  • Adaptability to new systems and technologies

Technical Skills

Applicants should have:

  • Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications
  • Ability to quickly learn new software and systems

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Working knowledge of English and/or French

For most internship opportunities:

  • Excellent English drafting and communication skills are required

Additional knowledge of WIPO working languages is considered an advantage:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • French
  • German
  • Japanese
  • Korean
  • Portuguese
  • Russian
  • Spanish

Diversity and Inclusion

WIPO strongly values diversity and equal opportunity.

The organization particularly encourages applications from:

  • Underrepresented regions
  • Unrepresented WIPO member states
  • Diverse cultural and geographic backgrounds

This makes the programme especially valuable for international students and graduates seeking global exposure.

Travel and Accommodation Information

Interns are responsible for:

  • Travel arrangements
  • Visa processing
  • Accommodation
  • Living expenses

However, WIPO provides limited travel reimbursement support for eligible applicants.

Travel Reimbursement

Interns from:

  • Developing countries
  • Least developed countries
  • Transition countries

may receive:

  • Round-trip travel reimbursement
  • Up to 1,500 Swiss Francs

This applies to candidates residing in their home countries at the time of recruitment.

Selection Process

The WIPO Internship Roster is not linked to one specific internship vacancy.

Instead:

  • Applications are stored in a roster database
  • Profiles are reviewed when suitable internship opportunities arise
  • Selected applicants are contacted for interviews

Interview Methods

The selection process may include:

  • Telephone interviews
  • Video interviews
  • In-person interviews
  • Written assessments
  • Pre-recorded video tasks

Due to the large volume of applications:

  • Only shortlisted candidates are contacted

If applicants are not contacted within six months after the deadline, their application was either unsuccessful or no suitable position matched their profile.

Required Application Documents

Applicants are expected to submit:

  1. Online application
  2. Cover letter or motivation letter
  3. Academic information
  4. Professional background details
  5. Supporting documentation where applicable

The motivation letter is an important component of the application process.

Application Deadline

July 3, 2026
9:59 PM

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully tailor their applications to their fields of expertise and career interests.

Click HERE to start your application

Why You Should Apply for the WIPO Internship Program

The WIPO Internship Program is one of the most competitive and respected international internship programmes within the United Nations system.

Participants gain:

  • International work experience
  • Exposure to global policymaking
  • Professional mentorship
  • Networking opportunities
  • UN system experience
  • Career development
  • Practical skills
  • Multicultural collaboration experience

For students and graduates interested in international law, innovation, intellectual property, public policy, technology, economics, or international development, this internship can significantly strengthen future academic and professional opportunities.

Click HERE to learn more about the WIPO Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland.

Conclusion

The WIPO Internship Program 2026 provides a remarkable opportunity for students and young professionals worldwide to work within a globally recognized international organization in Switzerland.

With competitive stipends, international exposure, professional development opportunities, and experience within the United Nations system, the programme offers a strong foundation for future careers in law, policy, technology, development, communications, administration, and intellectual property.

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the July 3, 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Internship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post United Nations WIPO Internship Program 2026 in Switzerland: Paid International Internship Opportunities for Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme 2026 for ASEAN Professionals | Fully Funded Sustainable Green Finance Training in Japan
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) has officially launched applications for the Green Economic Acceleration: A Japan–ASEAN Strategic Programme for Sustainable Green Finance, a prestigious fully funded regional training programme designed to strengthen sustainable finance leadership and green economic transformation across ASEAN countries. Supported by the Government and the People of Japan, […]

The post UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme 2026 for ASEAN Professionals | Fully Funded Sustainable Green Finance Training in Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) has officially launched applications for the Green Economic Acceleration: A Japan–ASEAN Strategic Programme for Sustainable Green Finance, a prestigious fully funded regional training programme designed to strengthen sustainable finance leadership and green economic transformation across ASEAN countries.

Supported by the Government and the People of Japan, the programme will equip government officials, private-sector professionals, entrepreneurs, and sustainability leaders with practical knowledge and advanced skills in sustainable finance, green investment, carbon markets, energy transition, and climate-related economic development.

The programme will run from July 2026 to March 2027 through a three-phase learning structure that combines online learning, advanced thematic training, expert mentoring, project development, and an international summit in Japan.

Participation in the programme is completely free of charge.

About the UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme

The Green Economic Acceleration Programme is a strategic Japan-ASEAN initiative created to strengthen regional cooperation in:

  • Sustainable finance
  • Green economic growth
  • Climate resilience
  • Clean energy investment
  • Carbon market development
  • Green infrastructure
  • Environmental sustainability

The programme aims to support professionals in ASEAN countries to develop practical, bankable green economy projects capable of driving sustainable development and regional economic resilience.

Participants will engage in a highly interactive learning experience featuring:

  • Self-paced online learning
  • Expert-led sessions
  • International peer exchange
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Capstone project development
  • Networking with Japanese institutions and industry leaders

The programme is organized by UNITAR, the dedicated training arm of the United Nations, known globally for strengthening institutional and individual capacities through high-quality training and innovative learning initiatives.

Programme Duration and Structure

The programme will take place between:

July 2026 – March 2027

It is divided into three progressively selective phases.

Phase I: Online Learning Programme

Duration:

July to September 2026

The first phase is open to all accepted participants.

During this stage, participants will complete self-paced online learning focused on:

  • Sustainable finance
  • Green economy systems
  • Climate-related economic transformation
  • Japan-ASEAN green cooperation

Activities Include:

  • Online learning modules
  • Expert webinars
  • Interactive peer discussions
  • Participation in an online learning community
  • Submission of an early-stage capstone project idea

This phase is designed to provide foundational understanding and practical exposure to sustainable economic development strategies.

Phase II: Advanced Thematic Training and Expert Mentoring

Duration:

October to December 2026

Only the top 200 performing participants from Phase I will advance to this stage.

Participants will receive specialized advanced training in one of four major thematic tracks:

Thematic Areas:

  1. Energy Transition
  2. Carbon Markets and Emission Trading
  3. Sustainable Banking and Green Fintech
  4. Green Transformation Policy

Activities Include:

  • Advanced expert-led training
  • Peer-to-peer collaboration
  • Mentorship from sustainability specialists
  • Development of practical green finance projects
  • Capstone refinement and strategic project planning

This phase focuses heavily on helping participants transform their ideas into practical and investable green economy solutions.

Phase III: Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit in Japan

Date:

February 2027

The top 50 participants from Phase II will be selected to travel to Japan for the prestigious Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit.

This phase provides participants with international exposure and direct engagement opportunities with Japanese institutions, investors, and sustainability stakeholders.

Summit Activities Include:

  • Project presentations and pitching sessions
  • Networking with industry leaders
  • Engagement with Japanese institutions
  • Study visits to enterprises in Japan
  • Partnership-building opportunities
  • Investment-focused discussions

The summit represents a major opportunity for participants to showcase their green economy project ideas before international stakeholders.

What Participants Will Learn

By the end of the programme, participants will gain advanced expertise and practical skills in multiple areas of sustainable development and green finance.

Key Learning Outcomes Include:

1. Green Finance and Investment Skills

Participants will learn how to:

  • Design bankable green economy projects
  • Develop advanced financing structures
  • Mobilize sustainable investment
  • Create climate finance solutions

2. Climate Policy and Technical Expertise

Participants will strengthen their understanding of:

  • Climate policy frameworks
  • Carbon markets
  • Sustainable infrastructure
  • Regional and global sustainability standards

3. Leadership and Regional Collaboration

The programme also develops:

  • Strategic leadership skills
  • Cross-border collaboration capabilities
  • Regional partnership-building expertise
  • Japan-ASEAN cooperation strategies

Programme Assignments and Expectations

Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the programme.

Assignments Include:

Phase I

  • Complete all online modules
  • Submit an early-stage capstone project proposal

Phase II

  • Improve and expand the capstone project
  • Participate in mentoring and peer-learning sessions

Phase III

  • Present and pitch the project during the Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applicants from ASEAN countries working in government, finance, sustainability, and related sectors.

Eligible Applicants Include:

Government Officials

Professionals working in ministries or agencies related to:

  • Finance
  • Economy
  • Energy
  • Environment
  • Trade
  • Sustainable development
  • Planning

Private-Sector Professionals

Applicants from industries such as:

  • Banking
  • Fintech
  • Infrastructure
  • Sustainability
  • Energy
  • Technology
  • Investment
  • Consulting
  • Industry
  • Project development

Preferred Areas of Experience

Applicants with experience or strong interest in the following fields are especially encouraged to apply:

  • Energy transition
  • Carbon markets
  • Emission trading
  • Sustainable banking
  • Green fintech
  • Green transformation policy
  • Climate finance
  • Public-private partnerships

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be from an ASEAN country
  • Demonstrate interest or experience in sustainable development fields
  • Be proficient in English
  • Have reliable internet access
  • Be computer literate
  • Be willing to participate fully in programme activities
  • Commit to completing all assignments and learning requirements

Certificates and Recognition

Participants who successfully complete Phase I will receive a:

Certificate of Completion

Participants advancing to later phases will receive additional certificates based on their participation.

Financial Coverage

The programme is offered:

Completely Free of Charge

Thanks to financial support from the Government and People of Japan.

Selected Phase III participants traveling to Japan will benefit from international exposure and summit participation opportunities.

Why This Programme Matters

As ASEAN countries continue transitioning toward greener economies, there is growing demand for professionals capable of designing innovative sustainable finance and climate investment solutions.

This programme provides a rare opportunity to:

  • Gain practical green finance expertise
  • Develop internationally relevant sustainability skills
  • Collaborate with regional and Japanese experts
  • Build investable green projects
  • Expand professional networks across ASEAN and Japan
  • Strengthen leadership in climate and sustainability sectors

For professionals interested in climate finance, energy transition, carbon markets, and sustainable economic transformation, this programme offers a valuable platform for long-term career growth and regional impact.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

30 June 2026 (11:59 p.m. JST / UTC+9)

Late submissions may not be accepted.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply online through the official UNITAR application portal.

Apply Here

Read More

About UNITAR

Established in 1965, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is the training and capacity-building arm of the United Nations.

UNITAR works globally to strengthen skills, institutional capacity, and leadership for sustainable development through innovative learning programmes, partnerships, and professional training initiatives aligned with the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

For more global Fellowship opportunities click here

The post UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme 2026 for ASEAN Professionals | Fully Funded Sustainable Green Finance Training in Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026: Fully Funded European Research Collaboration Opportunity for PhD Students and Postdoctoral Researchers
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers from Germany, France, and Poland to collaborate on innovative research projects addressing Europe’s most pressing challenges. Funded by the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space and supported through the German Academic Exchange Service […]

The post Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026: Fully Funded European Research Collaboration Opportunity for PhD Students and Postdoctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers from Germany, France, and Poland to collaborate on innovative research projects addressing Europe’s most pressing challenges.

Funded by the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space and supported through the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD), this prestigious initiative seeks to strengthen trilateral scientific cooperation while empowering the next generation of European researchers.

The programme creates a dynamic platform where PhD students and postdoctoral researchers can exchange ideas, build international research networks, and contribute to solutions shaping the future of Europe.

About the Future Network Weimar Triangle Programme

The Weimar Triangle was established in 1991 by Germany, France, and Poland to deepen cooperation and strengthen European unity. Since its creation, the partnership has served as a strategic framework for political, cultural, and scientific collaboration among the three countries.

The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme extends this vision into the research and innovation sector by bringing together outstanding young researchers from participating nations.

The initiative focuses on interdisciplinary collaboration and aims to develop long-term partnerships capable of tackling critical global and European challenges through science, technology, and innovation.

Participants will engage in trilateral research exchanges, academic cooperation, and international networking opportunities designed to foster impactful research outcomes and stronger European scientific resilience.

Research Areas and Priority Themes

Applicants are expected to conduct research aligned with one of the programme’s future-oriented thematic areas.

1. Climate-Neutral Energy and Climate Protection

This area focuses on sustainable technologies and solutions that support Europe’s climate ambitions.

Research topics may include:

  • Fusion technologies
  • Renewable energy expansion
  • Energy storage systems
  • Decarbonisation strategies
  • Sustainable mobility
  • Cross-border energy infrastructure
  • Climate protection technologies

2. Artificial Intelligence, Digitalisation, and Microelectronics

Researchers working in digital innovation and emerging technologies are strongly encouraged to apply.

Possible research interests include:

  • Artificial intelligence applications
  • Ethical AI standards
  • Cybersecurity
  • Europe’s digital sovereignty
  • Advanced microelectronics
  • AI-enabled chip technologies
  • Digital infrastructure innovation

3. Security in Europe and Research System Resilience

The programme supports research that enhances Europe’s strategic preparedness and resilience.

Relevant topics include:

  • Knowledge security
  • Research data protection
  • Countering hybrid threats
  • Risk management systems
  • Critical raw materials security
  • Strategic technologies
  • Science communication and public trust

4. Biotechnology and Medicine of the Future

This theme encourages innovative scientific work aimed at improving health systems, agriculture, and biotechnology solutions.

Research areas may include:

  • Sustainable health research
  • Medical technologies
  • Bioeconomy innovations
  • Innovative therapies and diagnostics
  • Food system resilience
  • Agricultural biotechnology

5. Social Sciences and Humanities Perspectives on Technology

The programme also recognizes the importance of understanding how technological transformations impact society.

Potential topics include:

  • Human-machine interaction
  • Societal implications of new technologies
  • Governance and democracy in the digital era
  • Ethical technology development
  • Social adaptation to emerging technologies

Benefits of the Future Network Weimar Triangle Programme

Selected participants will benefit from numerous academic and professional opportunities, including:

International Research Collaboration

Participants will work closely with researchers and institutions from Germany, France, and Poland, strengthening international academic cooperation.

Expanded Professional Networks

The programme enables researchers to build strong European networks that may support future research partnerships and career opportunities.

Exposure to High-Level Research Environments

Applicants gain access to leading institutions, experts, and innovative scientific communities across Europe.

Contribution to European Innovation

Researchers will directly contribute to solving major European and global challenges through interdisciplinary scientific collaboration.

Career Development Opportunities

The programme supports early-career researchers in enhancing their academic visibility, research experience, and international competitiveness.

Why This Programme Matters

Europe is currently facing major transitions in technology, climate policy, healthcare, energy systems, and security. The Future Network Weimar Triangle programme seeks to ensure that young researchers are actively involved in shaping solutions for these challenges.

By encouraging cooperation among France, Germany, and Poland, the programme strengthens scientific diplomacy and promotes long-term European resilience through research and innovation.

The initiative also aligns with the objectives of Germany’s High-Tech Agenda and the coalition agreement “Responsibility for Germany,” especially in areas related to education, research, and technological advancement.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to:

  • PhD students from France and Poland
  • Exceptionally qualified postdoctoral researchers from France and Poland
  • Researchers connected to participating institutions in Germany, France, and Poland

Applicants should demonstrate strong academic potential and research interests connected to the programme’s thematic priorities.

Application Process

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply through the official DAAD Future Network Weimar Triangle platform.

Applicants may also participate in the programme survey to receive updates and additional information regarding application procedures, programme activities, and future announcements.

Deadline

No official application deadline was specified in the provided information. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible and monitor the official programme page for updates.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme represents an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers seeking international collaboration, interdisciplinary engagement, and impactful scientific work.

For PhD students and postdoctoral researchers passionate about solving Europe’s future challenges, this programme offers a valuable platform to contribute to meaningful innovation while building lasting international academic partnerships.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026: Fully Funded European Research Collaboration Opportunity for PhD Students and Postdoctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University (Fall 2026): Research in Structural Engineering, Advanced Materials, Automated Construction, and AI-Assisted Simulation
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Pennsylvania State University (Penn State) is inviting applications from highly motivated and research-driven students for fully funded PhD positions beginning in Fall 2026 at its University Park campus. These doctoral opportunities will be supervised by Dr. Botong Zheng, who holds appointments in both the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Department of […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University (Fall 2026): Research in Structural Engineering, Advanced Materials, Automated Construction, and AI-Assisted Simulation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pennsylvania State University (Penn State) is inviting applications from highly motivated and research-driven students for fully funded PhD positions beginning in Fall 2026 at its University Park campus. These doctoral opportunities will be supervised by Dr. Botong Zheng, who holds appointments in both the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Department of Architectural Engineering.

The positions are expected to be fully funded and will provide competitive graduate assistantship packages for successful candidates.

This opportunity is designed for students who are passionate about conducting cutting-edge research at the intersection of structural engineering, advanced materials, automated construction technologies, computational modeling, large-scale experimental testing, and artificial intelligence-assisted simulation.

About the Research Group

Dr. Botong Zheng’s research group focuses on solving complex engineering challenges by integrating experimental research, computational techniques, advanced materials, and intelligent technologies.

The group aims to pursue pioneering and impactful research rather than incremental studies focused solely on publication output. Students joining the group will have the opportunity to work on innovative projects that advance the future of infrastructure, construction technologies, materials engineering, and structural systems.

Research activities may span multiple disciplines and involve collaborations with researchers, institutions, and federally funded programs across the United States and internationally.

What the Fully Funded PhD Position Offers

Successful applicants can expect access to numerous academic and research benefits, including:

Fully Funded Doctoral Studies

The selected PhD students will receive:

  • Full financial support for doctoral studies.
  • Competitive graduate assistantship packages.
  • Funding support associated with research projects.
  • Access to Penn State’s world-class research facilities and laboratories.
  • Opportunities to collaborate with leading researchers and industry partners.

High-Impact Research Environment

Students will engage in research that combines:

  • Structural engineering.
  • Mechanics and material behavior.
  • Advanced construction technologies.
  • Artificial intelligence applications.
  • Computational simulation.
  • Infrastructure resilience and performance evaluation.

The research environment is designed to help students build deep technical expertise while addressing real-world engineering challenges.

Multidisciplinary Research Opportunities

Depending on project alignment and funding availability, students may participate in:

  • Federally funded research initiatives.
  • Multi-institutional collaborations.
  • Interdisciplinary engineering projects.
  • Large-scale infrastructure testing programs.
  • Advanced manufacturing and automated construction studies.

Research Areas and Technical Focus

The research group is particularly interested in students who possess strong preparation in one or both of the following areas.

Experimental, Fabrication, and Hands-On Research

Students interested in experimental research will contribute to projects involving advanced materials, automated construction systems, and infrastructure-scale testing.

Relevant experience may include:

  • Structural laboratory testing.
  • Experimental design and setup.
  • Instrumentation systems.
  • Data acquisition systems.
  • Automated fabrication technologies.
  • Material development and characterization.
  • Infrastructure testing and validation.
  • Building and bridge engineering experiments.
  • Aerospace structural testing.
  • Manufacturing process development.

Candidates with practical laboratory experience and strong problem-solving abilities will be particularly competitive.

Computational Modeling, Programming, and AI-Assisted Simulation

Students interested in computational research will work on advanced modeling and simulation techniques related to materials, structures, and construction systems.

Relevant expertise may include:

  • Programming and software development.
  • Finite element modeling.
  • Computational mechanics.
  • Numerical simulation.
  • Artificial intelligence and machine learning applications.
  • Digital twin technologies.
  • Infrastructure performance prediction.
  • Material behavior simulation.
  • Construction process modeling.
  • Data-driven engineering methods.

Applicants with strong coding skills and experience using computational tools will be highly valued.

Research Topics Students May Explore

Potential research directions may include:

  • Advanced structural materials.
  • Automated construction and robotic fabrication.
  • Large-scale structural testing.
  • Smart infrastructure systems.
  • AI-assisted engineering simulations.
  • Computational mechanics.
  • Infrastructure resilience under extreme environments.
  • Material microstructure modeling.
  • Long-term infrastructure performance.
  • Data-driven structural engineering.
  • Sustainable construction technologies.
  • Intelligent monitoring systems.

Expectations for PhD Students

The research group seeks students who are committed to developing into independent researchers and future leaders in academia, industry, or government research organizations.

All PhD students are expected to develop strong capabilities in:

Research Analysis

Students should be capable of:

  • Designing rigorous research methodologies.
  • Analyzing complex datasets.
  • Drawing evidence-based conclusions.
  • Testing and validating hypotheses.

Technical Communication

Students must develop skills in:

  • Scientific writing.
  • Journal paper preparation.
  • Conference presentations.
  • Technical reporting.
  • Research dissemination.

Independent Research

Candidates should be prepared to:

  • Learn new technical skills quickly.
  • Work independently.
  • Solve open-ended research problems.
  • Persist through uncertainty and technical challenges.
  • Contribute original ideas to research projects.

The group emphasizes intellectual curiosity, technical excellence, and long-term impact rather than simply accumulating credentials or publication counts.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for students who:

  • Want to pursue a research-intensive PhD.
  • Aspire to become experts in structural engineering and advanced technologies.
  • Enjoy solving challenging engineering problems.
  • Have strong analytical and technical skills.
  • Are interested in both theory and practical implementation.
  • Seek to contribute to innovations that can transform infrastructure and construction industries.

The position is especially suitable for applicants interested in bridging the gap between experimental engineering, computational science, and artificial intelligence.

Application Process

Because the regular Fall 2026 graduate admission deadline has already passed, interested applicants must first complete the research group screening process.

Candidates should submit materials through the research group screening form before being considered for the next stage.

Required Application Materials

Applicants must provide:

Completed Screening Form

All questions in the group screening form must be completed accurately and thoroughly.

Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Submit a PDF-only file named:

LastName_FirstName_CV.pdf

The CV should include:

  • Educational background.
  • Research experience.
  • Technical skills.
  • Publications (if applicable).
  • Awards and honors.
  • Professional achievements.
  • Relevant academic activities.

Statement of Research Interests

Submit a PDF-only file named:

LastName_FirstName_SRI.pdf

This document is considered one of the most important parts of the application.

The statement should be no longer than two pages and clearly explain:

  • Your research interests.
  • Your preferred research direction.
  • Relevant technical skills.
  • Previous research experiences.
  • Academic strengths.
  • Career goals.
  • Why you are interested in joining Dr. Zheng’s research group.
  • How your background aligns with the group’s research focus.

Applicants are encouraged to provide specific examples that demonstrate their readiness for advanced doctoral research.

Why Penn State University Park?

Penn State’s University Park campus is one of the leading research universities in the United States, offering outstanding facilities, interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities, and a vibrant academic environment.

Students joining this research group will gain access to:

  • State-of-the-art laboratories.
  • Leading faculty mentorship.
  • Large-scale research infrastructure.
  • National and international collaborations.
  • High-impact research opportunities.
  • Strong professional development pathways.

For students seeking a challenging and rewarding doctoral experience in structural engineering, advanced materials, automated construction, and AI-assisted simulation, this fully funded PhD opportunity represents an excellent pathway toward a successful research career.

Application Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible through the group screening process due to the competitive nature of the opportunity.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University.

Explore more fully funded PhD opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University (Fall 2026): Research in Structural Engineering, Advanced Materials, Automated Construction, and AI-Assisted Simulation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute. This fully funded doctoral scholarship is designed for highly qualified candidates interested in cognitive computational neuroscience, decision-making, neural network modelling, and human brain research. The scholarship offers the chance to work under expert supervision in […]

The post Fully Funded Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute. This fully funded doctoral scholarship is designed for highly qualified candidates interested in cognitive computational neuroscience, decision-making, neural network modelling, and human brain research.

The scholarship offers the chance to work under expert supervision in a world-class research environment and develop advanced skills in computational modelling, empirical research, and scientific publication.

Scholarship Overview

The Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship is a four-year, fully funded program commencing in September or October 2026. It is attached to the Hamilton Institute and supervised by Dr Peter Murphy from the Department of Psychology.

Project Title: Identifying the Neurocomputational Basis for Making Decisions Abstracted from Sensation and Action

The scholarship supports a PhD candidate to investigate how the human brain forms abstract decisions independent of specific sensory input or motor actions. This work combines cognitive neuroscience, psychology, artificial intelligence, and computational modelling.

Benefits of the Scholarship

The Maynooth University Doctoral Scholarship provides:

  • Annual student stipend of €25,000
  • Full tuition fee support for four years
  • Research training through Maynooth University’s Graduate Research Academy
  • Opportunities to present research at local, national, and international conferences
  • Support in preparing peer-reviewed journal publications
  • Hands-on experience in neural network modelling, EEG studies, and computational neuroscience

About the Research Project

The project focuses on understanding the brain signal that reflects supramodal accumulation of information over time. This process allows humans to make decisions abstracted from immediate sensory or motor cues.

The PhD research will follow three major directions:

  1. Trained Neural Networks – Identify conditions under which abstract information accumulation emerges.
  2. Biologically Constrained Network Modelling – Investigate plausible neural implementations through computational models informed by biology.
  3. Empirical Testing in Humans – Validate model predictions using behavioural and EEG measurements of abstract decision signals.

Responsibilities of the PhD Student

The successful candidate will:

  • Conduct computational research using neural network models
  • Design and analyse EEG studies and behavioural experiments
  • Collect, manage, and analyse research data according to FAIR principles
  • Present findings at conferences and local meetings
  • Prepare draft publications for peer-reviewed journals
  • Undertake relevant training and engage with research development programs
  • Work independently and collaboratively under supervision

Mode of Study

  • Full-time residency at Maynooth University in Ireland for four years
  • Research primarily based at Maynooth University campus
  • Research outside campus requires supervisor and Head of Department permission
  • Subject to Maynooth University Doctoral Scholarship terms and Research Student Programme regulations

Eligibility Criteria

Essential Requirements:

  • First class or upper second class 2.1 honours undergraduate degree in Neuroscience, Cognitive Science, Computer Science, Psychology, or related discipline
  • Independent research experience with deep learning, neural networks, behavioural data, or EEG studies
  • Proficiency in quantitative analysis
  • Excellent written and oral communication in English

Desirable Qualifications:

  • Master’s degree in a relevant field with a significant research component
  • Computational skills (Python, MATLAB)
  • Familiarity with neural network platforms (TensorFlow, PyTorch)
  • Experience with GPU or cluster-based computing
  • Experience with human psychophysics and scalp EEG studies

Application Process

Applicants must submit:

  • Personal statement (max 600 words) highlighting motivation and experience
  • Curriculum Vitae including education, research experience, quantitative and computational skills, publications
  • Relevant academic transcripts
