  • Names and contact details of two academic referees

Submission: Email applications to Dr Peter Murphy at Peter.Murphy@mu.ie with the subject line:
Doctoral Scholarship – Hamilton Institute/Psychology 2026

Deadline: 5pm on 1 July 2026

Note: Non-native English speakers must provide proof of English proficiency. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Fully Funded Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO Media and Information Literacy Alliance Launches First Call for Chapter Creation 2026: Global Opportunity to Build Regional MIL Networks and Strengthen Media Literacy Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) Media and Information Literacy (MIL) Alliance has officially launched its first global call for the creation of MIL Alliance Chapters. This landmark initiative marks a major step toward strengthening collaboration, inclusivity, and localized action in media and information literacy across the world. The MIL Alliance is […]

The post UNESCO Media and Information Literacy Alliance Launches First Call for Chapter Creation 2026: Global Opportunity to Build Regional MIL Networks and Strengthen Media Literacy Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) Media and Information Literacy (MIL) Alliance has officially launched its first global call for the creation of MIL Alliance Chapters. This landmark initiative marks a major step toward strengthening collaboration, inclusivity, and localized action in media and information literacy across the world.

The MIL Alliance is designed as a “network of networks,” bringing together experts, institutions, and organizations committed to advancing media and information literacy. With this new chapter system, UNESCO is expanding opportunities for members to organize into strategic sub-groups based on shared interests, regions, or thematic priorities.

These chapters will enable more effective, context-specific interventions to address misinformation, improve digital literacy, and strengthen responsible communication globally.

What is the UNESCO MIL Alliance Chapter Initiative?

The MIL Alliance Chapter system is a structural innovation introduced by UNESCO to improve how its global members collaborate and implement media literacy initiatives.

Key Purpose of the Chapters

The chapters are designed to:

  • Strengthen regional and thematic collaboration among MIL Alliance members
  • Promote localized solutions to media and information literacy challenges
  • Overcome linguistic and cultural barriers in information access
  • Support advocacy for institutionalizing MIL policies with governments
  • Increase outreach to underrepresented communities
  • Foster collective ownership of MIL initiatives worldwide

These chapters represent an operational expansion of the MIL Alliance, making its global mission more practical, decentralized, and inclusive.

Why This Initiative Matters Globally

Media and information literacy has become essential in an era dominated by digital content, misinformation, and rapid technological change. UNESCO’s initiative directly addresses these challenges by empowering local and regional actors.

Major global impacts include:

  • Improved ability to identify misinformation and fake news
  • Stronger education systems integrating media literacy
  • Empowerment of youth as critical thinkers and responsible communicators
  • Enhanced policy advocacy at national and regional levels
  • Better collaboration across continents and cultural contexts

This initiative is particularly significant for regions like Africa, Latin America, the Caribbean, and other underserved areas where access to structured media literacy programs is still developing.

Structure and Governance of MIL Alliance Chapters

The MIL Alliance operates under a structured governance system to ensure transparency and accountability.

Key Governance Features

  • Chapters are reviewed and validated by the Global Board
  • The Global Board provides strategic oversight and coordination
  • UNESCO works closely with the Board in guiding implementation
  • Chapters must meet eligibility criteria and submit structured proposals
  • Reporting and development of chapters are continuously monitored

The Global Board was recently established through a global election process and now serves as the main decision-making body for the Alliance.

Who Can Apply for MIL Alliance Chapter Creation?

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be members of the UNESCO MIL Alliance
  • Represent organizations, institutions, or expert groups involved in media and information literacy
  • Demonstrate capacity to coordinate or contribute to MIL-related initiatives
  • Show alignment with UNESCO’s values of inclusion, education, and information access
  • Have interest in regional or thematic collaboration

Geographic Eligibility

This opportunity is open globally, including:

  • Africa
  • Europe
  • Asia
  • Latin America and the Caribbean
  • North America
  • Oceania

Other Eligibility Notes

  • Open to all races and ethnic backgrounds
  • Applicants must be part of the MIL Alliance network (currently spanning 117 countries)
  • Both institutional and expert-led groups may apply depending on guidelines

Application Deadline

The first call for MIL Alliance Chapter creation is open until:

31 July 2026

After this period, calls will be issued every two years for a duration of two months.

Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive and global nature of the review process.

Application Process Overview

Interested MIL Alliance members are encouraged to:

  1. Review official UNESCO MIL Alliance chapter guidelines
  2. Identify thematic or regional focus areas
  3. Prepare a structured chapter proposal
  4. Submit application through the official UNESCO MIL Alliance platform
  5. Await review and validation by the Global Board

Key Benefits of Joining or Creating a Chapter

  • Opportunity to influence global media literacy policy
  • Access to UNESCO’s international MIL network
  • Increased visibility for regional initiatives
  • Collaboration with experts across 117 countries
  • Capacity building in media literacy education
  • Contribution to global efforts against misinformation
  • Strengthened institutional partnerships

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNESCO Media and Information Literacy Alliance Launches First Call for Chapter Creation 2026: Global Opportunity to Build Regional MIL Networks and Strengthen Media Literacy Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) at IDOS Germany 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Food Systems, Sustainability, and Behavioral Research
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) has officially opened applications for an exciting Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) opportunity under the internationally recognized FoodDiverse research initiative. This position presents a remarkable opportunity for aspiring researchers and future academics who want to combine professional research experience with a fully supported PhD journey focused on sustainable food […]

The post Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) at IDOS Germany 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Food Systems, Sustainability, and Behavioral Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) has officially opened applications for an exciting Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) opportunity under the internationally recognized FoodDiverse research initiative. This position presents a remarkable opportunity for aspiring researchers and future academics who want to combine professional research experience with a fully supported PhD journey focused on sustainable food systems, nutrition, behavioral transformation, and development policy.

The doctoral position is hosted within the DFG-funded Collaborative Research Center (CRC 1780) “Food System Diversification for Sustainable Nutrition” (FoodDiverse), a major interdisciplinary research initiative jointly implemented by the University of Bonn, IDOS, and the University of Ghana.

Selected candidates will become part of a globally connected research environment examining how diversified food systems can improve nutrition, sustainability, and planetary health across Germany and Ghana.

The position is expected to begin on 01 October 2026 and will initially run for three years, with the possibility of an extension of up to nine additional months.

About the German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS)

The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) is one of Germany’s leading research institutions focused on development policy and sustainability studies. The institute is widely recognized for producing high-quality research, offering independent policy advice, and promoting international cooperation around sustainable futures.

IDOS combines academic excellence with practical policy engagement, creating a research environment where doctoral researchers can contribute directly to global conversations on sustainability, development, social transformation, and international cooperation.

Researchers at IDOS benefit from:

  • Internationally recognized mentorship
  • Participation in research workshops and conferences
  • Exposure to global policy discussions
  • Opportunities for international collaboration
  • Research stays abroad
  • Interdisciplinary teamwork
  • Structured doctoral supervision

Overview of the FoodDiverse Research Project

The selected doctoral researcher will join the FoodDiverse Collaborative Research Center, a major research consortium focused on understanding the relationship between food system diversification and sustainable nutrition.

The project brings together experts from various disciplines, including:

  • Agronomy
  • Sociology
  • Agricultural Economics
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Nutrition
  • Human Health
  • Ecology
  • Political Sciences
  • Anthropology

Researchers within the project investigate how agricultural diversity, market structures, and dietary patterns influence both human health and environmental sustainability.

The doctoral researcher will specifically contribute to the subproject titled:

“Behavioral Strategies to Diversify Food Consumption”

The research will focus heavily on:

  • Plant-based protein consumption
  • Food behavior transformation
  • Policy and discourse analysis
  • Sustainable nutrition systems
  • Social and cultural food practices
  • Consumer behavior in Germany and Ghana

Research Focus and PhD Structure

The doctoral project is embedded within the IDOS Dual PhD Programme, which combines advanced academic research with practical policy-oriented engagement.

The PhD research will involve empirical fieldwork in both Germany and Ghana, allowing the researcher to study food consumption patterns and behavioral transformation across different social and cultural contexts.

A major focus of the research includes:

  1. Investigating plant-based protein intake across social groups
  2. Exploring discourse around sustainable food systems
  3. Analyzing policy frameworks influencing food diversification
  4. Studying transformational consumption behavior
  5. Examining sustainable nutrition strategies

The doctoral candidate will be supervised by:

  • Prof. Dr. Anna-Katharina Hornidge
  • Jun.-Prof. Dr. Dominic Lemken
  • Prof. Dr. Monika Hartmann

The University of Bonn Faculty of Social Sciences will serve as the degree-awarding institution.

What Makes This PhD Opportunity Unique?

This opportunity goes beyond a standard doctoral position. It combines:

1. Academic Research Excellence

Researchers receive direct access to:

  • International conferences
  • Research workshops
  • High-level scientific mentoring
  • Collaborative interdisciplinary networks

2. Structured PhD Support

IDOS offers a semi-structured PhD programme including:

  • Group mentoring
  • Peer mentoring
  • One-on-one supervision
  • Research training activities
  • Dissertation support

At least 50% of the working time is dedicated exclusively to dissertation research.

3. International Fieldwork Experience

The project includes fieldwork in:

  • Ghana
  • Germany

This provides practical international research exposure and cross-cultural research experience.

4. Policy and Real-World Impact

The research contributes directly to ongoing discussions on:

  • Sustainable nutrition
  • Food security
  • Climate-sensitive diets
  • Development policy
  • Behavioral transformation

Job Responsibilities

The selected doctoral researcher will undertake several important responsibilities within the FoodDiverse project.

Main Tasks Include:

  • Developing an individual PhD research agenda
  • Designing conceptual and methodological research frameworks
  • Conducting empirical fieldwork in Ghana and Germany
  • Performing discourse and policy analysis
  • Collaborating with interdisciplinary research teams
  • Working closely with project partners in both countries
  • Participating actively in the IDOS Dual PhD Programme
  • Contributing to institutional activities at IDOS

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

Applicants must demonstrate strong academic and research potential.

Required Qualifications

Candidates should possess:

  • A Master’s degree in relevant disciplines such as:
    • Sociology
    • Human Geography
    • Political Science
    • Anthropology
    • Agricultural Sciences
    • Nutrition Sciences

Required Skills and Experience

Applicants should also demonstrate:

  • Knowledge of qualitative social science research methods
  • Experience with ethnographic research
  • Strong communication abilities
  • Excellent academic writing skills
  • Organizational competence
  • Motivation to pursue doctoral research
  • Ability to work in interdisciplinary teams

Additional Advantages

The following are considered strong assets:

  • Research or professional experience in Africa
  • Knowledge of German language
  • International collaboration experience

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Fluent written and spoken English

German language proficiency is beneficial but not mandatory.

Travel Requirement

Candidates must be willing to conduct international travel and fieldwork, including work in tropical climates.

Salary and Employment Benefits

The doctoral researcher position offers competitive compensation under the German public sector salary framework.

Salary Information

  • Salary Scale: EG 13 TVoeD Bund
  • Gross Salary Range: €40,000 – €58,000 annually (65% employment basis)

Working Hours

  • Part-time position
  • 65% employment
  • 25.4 working hours per week

Additional Employee Benefits

IDOS provides numerous professional and social benefits, including:

Work Flexibility

  • Flexible working hours
  • Hybrid work options
  • Home office possibilities within Germany (up to 60%)

Financial Benefits

  • Annual special payment
  • Employer pension contribution
  • Subsidized Deutschland Job Ticket

Leave and Vacation

  • Up to 30 vacation days annually
  • Additional company vacation days

Professional Development

  • Training opportunities
  • Career development support
  • International academic exposure

Diversity and Inclusion Commitment

IDOS actively promotes equality, diversity, and inclusion in its recruitment process.

Applications are strongly encouraged from:

  • Women
  • People of colour
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Individuals from all nationalities
  • Applicants with migration backgrounds
  • Diverse gender identities and sexual orientations

The institute is certified under the “workandfamily” audit program, demonstrating its commitment to work-life balance and inclusive employment practices.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare the following documents:

  1. Cover Letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Academic Certificates
  4. Supporting documentation

Candidates with foreign university degrees must additionally provide proof of degree recognition through the Anabin database excerpt recognized by the German Central Office for Foreign Education (ZAB).

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than:

14 June 2026 at 23:59 CET

Late applications may not be considered.

Interview Timeline

Shortlisted candidates are expected to participate in interviews scheduled for:

July 2026

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted exclusively through the official IDOS application portal.

Applicants should reference:

Position Number: 2026-B-02

Click here for more and to apply

Why You Should Apply

This doctoral researcher opportunity at IDOS offers much more than a traditional PhD position. It is a chance to:

  • Conduct internationally relevant sustainability research
  • Work alongside globally respected researchers
  • Gain interdisciplinary and international research experience
  • Influence policy discussions on sustainable nutrition
  • Develop advanced qualitative research expertise
  • Build a strong international academic network
  • Earn a competitive salary while pursuing a PhD

For aspiring researchers passionate about food systems, sustainability, behavioral transformation, and global development, this opportunity represents a major step toward an impactful international academic career.

For more global job opportunities click here

The post Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) at IDOS Germany 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Food Systems, Sustainability, and Behavioral Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
